mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and imminent recession, triggering risk-off capital flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high GDP growth and elevated consumer price inflation force central banks to implement aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression, or 60-day forward returns remain positive when both proxies are in the top quartile.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, driving short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next week during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal accuracy drops below 50% in post-shock recovery windows, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO momentum (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust consumer spending capacity and corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 3-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth coincides with negative 20-day equity returns in > 65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 900 thousand units signals a sharp contraction in residential construction and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the threshold condition fails to improve out-of-sample classification accuracy over a naive baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with decelerating consumer price inflation indicate a successful soft landing, stabilizing earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate models, or 60-day forward returns turn negative when rates rise alongside falling inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated retail sales figures during late-cycle expansions reflect peak consumer leverage and unsustainable demand, triggering mean-reversion dynamics that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal accuracy drops below 50% in late-cycle windows, or high retail sales consistently coincide with positive 5-day equity returns (Rank IC > 0.05).
